title: Silencing a War-Drum Rehearsal Hall
seed_problem: acoustic-panel placement under a resonant standing-wave field
mutation: composite: a periodically-driven rectangular hall is decomposed into resonance channels (standing-wave modes, plus one geometric first-reflection channel from the mirror-image construction); a weighted absorber-panel budget must be spent on wall positions whose damping benefit is proportional to that position's OWN modal amplitude, not the listener's -- coupling coefficients and listener position are decoupled from panel cost/strength so proximity to the listener carries no information.
strategies: trivial: install no panels (reproduces the checker's baseline B exactly)
naive greedy: install panels on wall cells nearest the listener(s), cheapest affordable first -- ignores the given per-channel coupling table entirely

insight (strong): use the input's own Delta/Gamma0/S/L table to weight each panel by its estimated marginal reduction of F on the currently most-vulnerable (smallest-denominator) channels, then run a marginal-gain (submodular) greedy that re-evaluates the true objective after each pick -- correctly finds that panels far from the listener, at a resonant mode's antinode near the drum, dominate the payoff
